parameters.description:

 - SEARCH_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: The default / unset value.
 - SEARCH_TYPE_PORTFOLIO: Fields searched - portfolioCode,portfolioTicker,portfolioFullname.
 - SEARCH_TYPE_CLIENT_NAME: Fields searched - clientName,clientEntityId,contractId.
 - SEARCH_TYPE_ALTERNATE_ID: Fields searched - portfolioCusip,cusip.
 - SEARCH_TYPE_EXTERNAL_ACCOUNT_NUMBER: Fields searched - externAcct,xrefClientCode.
 - SEARCH_TYPE_MARKET_ID: Fields searched - bbTicker,cins,cusip,isin,ric,sedol,ticker - in SecurityADL.
 - SEARCH_TYPE_PORTFOLIO_TYPE: Fields searched - portfolioType

OrderDelegationConsentOrderDelegationCon.description:
- ORDER_DELEGATION_CONSENT_OPTION_UNSPECIFIED: This Order Delegation Consent is not applicable
 - ORDER_DELEGATION_CONSENT_OPTION_YES: The Order Delegation Consent is set as TRUE
 - ORDER_DELEGATION_CONSENT_OPTION_NO: The Order Delegation Consent is set as FALSE
 - ORDER_DELEGATION_CONSENT_OPTION_SPECIFIC_INSTRUCTIONS: The Order Delegation Consent is set as SPECIFIC_INSTRUCTIONS
 - ORDER_DELEGATION_CONSENT_OPTION_PENDING_NOTIFICATION: This Order Delegation Consent is set as PENDING_NOTIFICATION
 - ORDER_DELEGATION_CONSENT_OPTION_PENDING_CONSENT: This Order Delegation Consent is set as PENDING_CONSENT
OrderDelegationConsentOrderDelegationCon.title: Describes portfolio order delegation consent options

PortfolioFlagPortfolioFlagOption.description:
- PORTFOLIO_FLAG_OPTION_UNSPECIFIED: This flag is not applicable for the portfolio
 - PORTFOLIO_FLAG_OPTION_YES: The flag is set as TRUE for the portfolio
 - PORTFOLIO_FLAG_OPTION_NO: The flag is set as FALSE for the portfolio
 - PORTFOLIO_FLAG_OPTION_CONDITIONAL: The flag is dependent on a condition in the contract
 - PORTFOLIO_FLAG_OPTION_SILENT: The flag is not explicitly defined in the contract
 - PORTFOLIO_FLAG_OPTION_UNDEFINED: The flag is set as UNDEFINED for the portfolio
PortfolioFlagPortfolioFlagOption.title: Describes portfolio flag options

`NullValue` is a singleton enumeration to represent the null value for the
`Value` type union.

 The JSON representation for `NullValue` is JSON `null`.

rpcStatus.description:
The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for
different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is
used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains
three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details.

You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the
[API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of [google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description:
A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any
user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the
[google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized by the client.

details.description:
A list of messages that carry the error details.  There is a common set of
message types for APIs to use.

pageSize.description:
The maximum number of Portfolios to return. The service may
return fewer than this value. If unspecified, at most X
Portfolios will be returned. The maximum value is Y; values
above Y will be coerced to Y.

pageToken.description:
A page token, received from a previous 'FilterPortfolios' call.
Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.

When paginating, all other parameters provided to
'FilterPortfolios' must match the call that provided the page
token.

If this field is null, then a default jql is taken from EXT_NOTIF_BQL in files.dat.
To publish all trades, put an identity like $cusip = $cusip.

typeDateTime.description:
* When utc_offset is set and time_zone is unset: a civil time on a calendar
   day with a particular offset from UTC.
 * When time_zone is set and utc_offset is unset: a civil time on a calendar
   day in a particular time zone.
 * When neither time_zone nor utc_offset is set: a civil time on a calendar
   day in local time.

The date is relative to the Proleptic Gregorian Calendar.

If year is 0, the DateTime is considered not to have a specific year. month
and day must have valid, non-zero values.

This type is more flexible than some applications may want. Make sure to
document and validate your application's limitations.
typeDateTime.title: Represents civil time in one of a few possible ways:

year.description:
Optional. Year of date. Must be from 1 to 9999, or 0 if specifying a
datetime without a year.

month.description: Required. Month of year. Must be from 1 to 12.

day.description:
Required. Day of month. Must be from 1 to 31 and valid for the year and
month.

hours.description:
Required. Hours of day in 24 hour format. Should be from 0 to 23. An API
may choose to allow the value "24:00:00" for scenarios like business
closing time.

minutes.description: Required. Minutes of hour of day. Must be from 0 to 59.

seconds.description:
Required. Seconds of minutes of the time. Must normally be from 0 to 59. An
API may allow the value 60 if it allows leap-seconds.

nanos.description:
Required. Fractions of seconds in nanoseconds. Must be from 0 to
999,999,999.

utcOffset.description:
UTC offset. Must be whole seconds, between -18 hours and +18 hours.
For example, a UTC offset of -4:00 would be represented as
{ seconds: -14400 }.

typeTimeOfDay.description:
Represents a time of day. The date and time zone are either not significant
or are specified elsewhere. An API may choose to allow leap seconds. Related
types are [google.type.Date][google.type.Date] and `google.protobuf.Timestamp`.

hours.description:
Hours of day in 24 hour format. Should be from 0 to 23. An API may choose
to allow the value "24:00:00" for scenarios like business closing time.

minutes.description: Minutes of hour of day. Must be from 0 to 59.

seconds.description:
Seconds of minutes of the time. Must normally be from 0 to 59. An API may
allow the value 60 if it allows leap-seconds.

nanos.description: Fractions of seconds in nanoseconds. Must be from 0 to 999,999,999.

typeTimeZone.description:
Represents a time zone from the
[IANA Time Zone Database](https://www.iana.org/time-zones).

id.description: IANA Time Zone Database time zone, e.g. "America/New_York".

version.description: Optional. IANA Time Zone Database version number, e.g. "2019a".

jql.description:
Represents BQL statement which, if true, lets the delivery be published.
If this field is null, then a default jql is taken from EXT_NOTIF_BQL in files.dat.
To publish all trades, put an identity like $cusip = $cusip.
If the BQL is a standard macro then the same BQL will also be present in misdb..jql_macro_def .def.
This is not a straight join since Extern_delivery.jql is just BQL. It can be any BQL expression, which can include macros if necessary.
